action.reasoning: Observation: Command error in iter 23 was from a test (not eval) that failed due to shape mismatch (outc.weight [2,128,1,1] vs model [2,256,1,1]) and some residual keys. The ensemble list includes checkpoints with potentially differing architectures (e.g., base_channels 64 vs 128, residual vs non-residual). The last successful eval (iter 18) used 6 compatible checkpoints and achieved 426.9984 rmse_z500. The current predict.py does not handle incompatible checkpoints gracefully; a load failure aborts the script. Analysis: We need robust loading: try-except to skip any checkpoint that fails to load (shape mismatch, missing/unexpected keys). This will allow the ensemble to proceed with all compatible checkpoints. The time budget is ~18 min, enough for eval. Decision: Update predict.py to skip incompatible checkpoints, then submit eval to possibly improve upon the best metric. No training needed.
